scenario_title: Delete apex name and leave zone apex invariants unspecified
test_rationale: This test sets up a minimal zone apex with the required SOA and NS plus one extra apex TXT rrset, then issues a single delete_name for the apex itself. That operation is the underspecified choice point: RFC 2136 does not say whether delete_name at the apex must be refused, must preserve mandatory rrsets, or may remove only non-mandatory apex data. The follow-up SOA, NS, and TXT queries make the difference observable both in the UPDATE reply and in the post-state.

group_1.behavior: Reject the apex delete_name UPDATE or preserve mandatory apex data while possibly retaining the extra TXT rrset.
group_1.post_state: SOA and NS remain at the apex; TXT may remain unchanged.

group_2.behavior: Accept the UPDATE differently, potentially deleting the apex TXT while preserving mandatory SOA/NS, or rejecting with a different rcode.
group_2.post_state: SOA and NS remain at the apex; TXT is removed.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 §3.4.2 defines delete_name but does not specify a special rule or mandatory error for deleting the zone apex. RFC 1035 and RFC 2181 §7 describe SOA/NS as structurally required at the apex, but they do not turn that into a precise UPDATE postcondition. As a result, implementations can differ in whether they reject the UPDATE, partially apply it, or preserve only mandatory apex rrsets, making both reply code and final apex contents implementation-dependent.
